utility_evaluation.REASONING:
There are 5 items in the required format: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. All 5 appear in the final coverage plan. Now check if each is fully specified with details and costs:
- health_coverage: Specifies physical and mental health insurance, each with plan names, monthly costs, some features, and a combined total monthly cost. Fully specified per format (physical and mental health insurance with costs).
- property_coverage: Specifies home/contents insurance with plan name, monthly cost, coverage limit, deductible detail, and total monthly cost. Fully specified per format.
- travel_coverage: Specifies travel insurance with base and adventure components, each with costs and coverage details, plus total monthly cost. Fully specified per format.
- additional_services: Specifies an enhancement service with cost and total additional cost. Fully specified per format.
- total_budget: Provides monthly and annual totals, and extra context. Fully specified per format (complete monthly/annual cost breakdown present).
Therefore, all 5/5 items are included and fully specified.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: I matched each item name in the final coverage plan to the closest exact entry in the ground-truth ratings. The plan includes: LifeFirst Standard Plan (physical health) which has a rating of 9 under primary health coverage -> physical_health; InsurePlus Standard Plan (mental health) which has a rating of 8 under primary health coverage -> mental_health; PrimeHome Premium Coverage for home contents which maps to property_protection -> home_content -> PrimeHome Premium Coverage with rating 6; PrimeTravel Premium Plan which maps to mobility_coverage -> travel_insurance -> PrimeTravel Premium Plan with rating 6; Adventure Travel Coverage which maps to travel_enhancement_services -> Adventure Travel Coverage with rating 10; Technology Protection which maps to property_enhancement_services -> Technology Protection with rating 6.
